submission.title: All 50 units and 100+ variations seen so far!

comments.body: Hi, I made a video collecting every unit and variation that I've been able to spot so far (50 units and more than 100 variations) in the trailer and other media showcases so we have access to the entire collection of revealed content in a single source. I hope you guys enjoy it and let me know if I missed anything! \^\^
Really incredible deep dive! Thanks!!

These are the same creatures I found, EXCEPT for that forest warden. Really good eyes!

I'm still not 100% on that Faun/Dryad unit being one unit family. The faun unit we see in game with a melee weapon has a brown colour scheme, but the official artwork where the Sylvans are fighting the undead have the melee weapon user in a green/red motif. But it could just be the artist's choice of colour palette. I'm excited to see the Sylvan lineup finally revealed lol, as they're the biggest question mark. Or at least show the town dwellings so we have an idea who's made the cut, lol!

Is it possible that the "8th unit per faction" theory is just them bringing back the choice between two ultimate units?  
Temple: Angel/Celestial Archer  
Sylvan: Gold Dragon/Faerie Dragon/Stone Elemental  
Necropolis: Bone Dragon/ Death Reaper  
New Faction: Baron Harkonnen/Flying Baron Nashor  
etc.?

Great work!

  
The evil eye model you found seems more mechanical than anything, which sounds like a neutral to me.  

  
I also suspect the forest warden may be an alternate upgrade to the druid, it seems like it has a similar frame and stance, and would form a theme with with the fauns of having melee upgrades for ranged units

  
With respect to the extra units, it feels like every faction is "sharing" a high tier unit with another faction.  The Bone dragon and the gold dragon clearly are based on the same model, and the alternate celestial archer seems like version that's been corrupted by the vori\*-similar colour schemes.  If true, those are the units that are most likely not in the standard lineup because of the cost cutting.
Do you guys think there will be Ubisoft to release the game? Their stock price is shocking

Eh, they have a new Assassins Creed, these always sell, they will survive at least until release. And if somehow not there will be someone who buys either the whole company or their IPs, and I think this game is far enough that they probably won't cancel it (there is of course always the possibility of everything going wrong but that's relatively unlikely, and if worse comes to worst there is going indie and doing a kickstarter to finish it).

Good job.

Wasn't there a Sylvan elf dancer?
No, she was just an alternate upgrade to the chakram dancer from Dungeon.
The problem is that they have the new Assassins Creed. Previous sold very well, but this time they decided to get all possible money in the world.
I see. So there's no elves in Sylvan roster at all?

As of now, it doesn't seem so. Maybe because we already have the dark elves in dungeon and the Vori elves (unconfirmed) in the new faction that they thought three elven factions would've been overkill. Looks like there might be elven heroes though, purely based on the official splash artwork with Sylvan fighting undead. But the units are largely within the fae/nature motif.

Still, all of these is speculation based on screenshots so we might be in for surprises once the faction is officially revealed.

The Vori are explicitely not from Jadame.  
They're from the island of Vori, which is just off the coast of Antagarich (the continent where Erathia is), and thats miles and miles south of Jadame. So there's no reason they'd be on Jadame especially considering they're rather isolationist.

The Dark Elves still don't look like Jadamean Dark elves, which is a problem. They look like Ashan dark elves right now. Which would be fine if the game were set on Ashan, but it isn't.

And yeah .... those Sylvan units give very hard Warcraft 3 vibes (and what did they turn the sprite into ????), not a good thing.

Very detailed.Looks liek you missed one . Theres a some kind of armoured centaur with bow in one screen.

[https://www.gematsu.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Heroes-of-Might-and-Magic-Olden-Era\_2024\_08-22-24\_012.jpg](https://www.gematsu.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Heroes-of-Might-and-Magic-Olden-Era_2024_08-22-24_012.jpg)

There are 2 main reasons why I think the fauns are all part of the same family: they're rigged exactly the same way (which is commonly a huge hint in game design) and the faction seems too crowded to make them 2 separate tiers.

Maybe the tier 7 is a choice as you mentioned, though I don't think they beholders would fit as a tier 7 and hydras are already shown as tier 6... but they can still be ballistas and maybe we get another unit!

Btw, I lol'd at the "baron harkonnen" name, very fitting indeed!

My bad, I thought the Vori migrated to the island but once were part of Jadame.

  
The Dark Elves looking more like Ashan ones I think is more an issue of art direction looking for modern designs maybe? Same as other creatures having updated designs.
That's actually a minotaur holding an axe! I was iffy on what it could be but there are shots of that same scene with less fog and you can see it's just a minotaur.

It's ok, mistakes happen :) ! I praise you regardless for going to the lengths of gathering all that info into one place :D !

It could be that, but it's still a very poor choice imo to go with the Ashan design (for all creatures who have them) because it both 1. doesn't fit the old universe (its entirely Ashan-coded, for obvious reasons), and 2. it's uninventive, because it's already been made .... for Ashan

Add to that the fact that even just for the elves themselves, MM8 gave a very solid basis to get creative with : Tan-to-Red skin, silvery eyes, black/white/grey hair. From there, armor designs could have gone almost anywhere, and the dark elves would still have been Enroth-coded, so fitting for the setting choice the devs went with. But as it is, if you look at them closely, they've got very long/large ears, which screams Warcraft 3, and are dressed in H6-7's assassin outfit, which as I mentioned, is obviously Ashan, not at all Enroth. The same issue with the warcraft-y-ness of the elves goes for Sylvan, they look like cenarion creatures from the Warcraft universe, mixed with the night elves, which is a very wrong move imo.

Oddly enough, it's the same kind of issue with creature redesigns in the next Dragon Age : the issue isn't with things looking different, but with things looking uninspired, unfitting in the pre-established universe, and overall worse.
All the elves on Jadame are dark elves, so that's fitting with the lore.

>The Dark Elves still don't look like Jadamean Dark elves, which is a problem. They look like Ashan dark elves right now. Which would be fine if the game were set on Ashan, but it isn't.

I fail to see how it's a problem. If we are taking MM8 as the base, then most factions should straight up be entierely reworked, there was no hive, no sylvan, the dungeon would be limited to the minotaurs being their own faction, the temple would be a completely insignificant faction with only some clerics, and I can continue for a bit.

HOMM and MM were only loosely connected, as things as they appears in either games were not reproduced into the other part of the game.

Remember bracada, the faction in HOMM 3 which is a snowy mountain and was translated into a desert in MM7?

Well yes, I personally think most factions should be, reworked and adapted to what we've been shown of Jadame. You could get some very unique and interesting takes there :

For your Sylvan ? Go with the Jadamean dark, the region around alvar is populated by wolves, wasp warriors, centaurs, combined with the dark elves (which whatever your opinion might be, should not look liek Ashan ones to me), and you get very fresh, darker take on Sylvan, with the more dangerous and wild aspect of nature

For your Dungeon ? Ravage roamings. Have the Minotaurs from balthazar's lair there allied with the local ogres, and taming the local wyverns and gorgons. Boom, a more bestial "horde" like Dungeon. You could even have the ally with the dragons of Garotte Gorge if the dark elves don't for the more classic Dungeon feel. And if you crave some snakelady in here, have them get the naga from Garotte gorge as well ?

For your Haven ? Pretty easy, and a way to make it actually unique : Gather the Church of the Sun from Murmurwood, with their clerics and light elementals, have them allly with the dragon hunters from Garotte gorge to be their armed force, and have the dragon hunters tame the unicorns and basilisk from Murmurwood. Boom, a uniquely Jadamean Haven.

And all of that you can dive into without having anything look like it's from Ashan. Because it f\*\*\*ing shouldn't.

HoMM and MM share the same world. Enough said.

If you think desert and snow are mutually exclusive, you should look up some mountain ranges in Africa.

All your points fall flat.
I didn't know that. Thankya.
Yup good about the hero.

... OK, then let’s keep going.


Angels are aligned with the wizards in MM7, not at all with Eratia. Bracada doesn’t have any snow in it, at all. "Look at africa" is not a decent rebuttal, we are talking about what we are shown in game here. In MM7, wizards are all desert and no snow, in HOMM3, the wizards are all snow and no desert (native terrain is snow, not snow and desert). They are also completely unrelated to titans, who are living close to... the elves and the kreegans.


Tatalia which is supposed to be to be the lands of lizardmen or gnolls, has zero gnoll, zero lizardman, zero dragon fly, zero basilisk, zero gorgon, zero wyver’ and zero hydra in MM7


I can keep going for hours.

Uhu ... And where do you see me complaining about mixing rosters weirdly, exactly ? It's literally what I did in my previous message.

Bracada (lorewise) has deserts in the north (like in MM7), and snowy mountains in the the highest parts and to the south.

All your example use HoMM3's own setting (common with MM6-7-8), as do mine. And I stuck to strictly the continent the devs have set the game in. The rosters I mention divert from the usual too. And none of them look like Ashan (which was my initial point, in case you forgot).

By all means, keep going if you're having fun, but so far none of what you say counters my initial point : things shouldn't look like their Ashan counterparts, or like Warcraft 3 creatures.

Or we shouldn’t feel forced to use a game over two decades old as a base design for a new 2025 game when the whole serie is plagued with discrepancies already. You know. Maybe.


Dunno, go look what a hobgoblin looks like in HOMM3, what it looks like in MM7, and keep crying a river about dark elves design.

Oh no one *should* feel forced to anything for sure. But I think the team letting their creatives go wild with new things (the ice faction is intriguing for one, even though I have zero clue at all what it might be) is more interesting than reusing the design from Ashan, which was already invented, obviously. And considering the love for the old universe/old games, if you decide, rather than stay on Ashan, or even write a new world of your own, to return to the old world that's well loved by a great number of fans, you kinda *have* to pay it a measure of respect and fidelity. Nothing needs to be identical, but it needs to be identifiable and be possible to relatae to the old.

Otherwise you're just using the name of the old universe to tug at people's nostalgia, and that's a very questionable tactic to get people to buy. Not a good look to have.
Looks great so far. Thanks for the video.

submission.title: I do wish that

comments.body: Frankly, why not both ?  I love the sci-fi elements of the might and magic games but I also think that the reckoning is a neat event and a fine conclusion to Enroth's story

A conclusion is an ending to which the events build up to. The devs ened Enroth because the worldbuilding was so succesful and deep that they got confused, so they decided to erase everything. so I don't think it was "fine", or a "conclusion" for that matter. In MM3, they said CRON and its VARNs were drowned in the sea of Terra. Players felt like their actions in MM2 didn't matter, so NWC decided to retcon it in MM6, saying it was a different CRON. Now take 7 games that takes place on Enroth: 2 succession wars, a restoration war with sequels and prequels, Chronicles, 3 successful attempts to avoid the destruction of the world, and the world is destroyed anyway, just because 2 idiots started swinging their tactical nukes like a sword.

>In MM3, they said CRON and its VARNs were drowned in the sea of Terra. Players felt like their actions in MM2 didn't matter, so NWC decided to retcon it in MM6, saying it was a different CRON.

I mean, I don't remember if it's implied to be the same CRON (I'm going through 2 right now) but in MM1 it's made clear that there's multiple VARNs and multiple CRONs

>Now take 7 games that takes place on Enroth: 2 succession wars, a restoration war with sequels and prequels, Chronicles, 3 successful attempts to avoid the destruction of the world, and the world is destroyed anyway, just because 2 idiots started swinging their tactical nukes like a sword.

Yeah. So basically everything telling "this will happens some day for sure" and it ends up happening.

If forge was present, I'm sure that something like the reckoning would have happened anyways

>Yeah. So basically everything telling "this will happens some day for sure" and it ends up happening.

But the effect is less "Ah this is the sad but satisfying conclusion to a tragedy" and more"well why did I even bother saving the world from Kreegans and Escaton and whatever else?"

I can see a version of these games where it builds better to the Reckoning than what we got.  Maybe if it was clearer in the various finales that "you've solved the immediate problems, but in a way that will have dire consequences later." Which they did with the release of Archibald in MM6, but he was never a world-ending threat.  If you're going to blow up your campaign setting, having it happen between games, done by characters who stopped being playable two or more entries ago with the setup being in a side game with a limited release (I never did find Sword of Frost back in the day) just makes it feel cheap and I obviously what it really is: a soft reboot.

That said...

>If forge was present, I'm sure that something like the reckoning would have happened anyways

... I absolutely believe this is correct.  The Reckoning was, first and foremost, an out of character decision to sweep away as much of the Enrothian setting as they liked, and the perceived problems that led to that decision would've almost certainly still happened.

Although, if the Forge was, at least in part, responsible for the Reckoning (say, attempting to destroy it without understanding how it works leads to a world-ending meltdown), I'd probably call that a more satisfying way to bring it about, since PC action (the Dark ending of MM7) were key to bringing it about.  Plus whatever "play as the new villains" scenarios would've been in Armageddon's Blade.

Is this thread a rant about a story 2 decades old? 


Guys, I know you are stuck in the past since you think Homm3 is the best thing which happened since sliced bread, but come on, try to move on.
I don't think H3 is the best thing, I just said I don't agree with the decision to blow up the whole setting.
Nah HoMM3 ain't best thing which happened since sliced bread that would be Age of Empires 2, its second best thing sharing space with Disciples 2.

Forge is blowing up the whole setting but in a different manner (worst, imo).

More seriously, the whole serie was a continuity mess, where nothing ever made any sense between each game. So yeah, at some point, they decided to blow up the thing in order to start anew properly, or they hoped to do so.

The Reckoning might have not happened simply because Gregory Fulton, the man behind the creation of Antagarich, might have still been working at NWC if the Forge was not canceled. Thus, they had to abandon Enroth for two reasons:

- No Gregory Fulton to work on Antagarich's future

- The creation of a new setting in Legends of Might and Magic (which had a really great scope to unify the series, but was killed by the publisher). Yes, it was meant to introduce Axeoth more properly.

I’m mainly a Scenario player, I don’t have Chronicles and I haven’t played through all the Campaigns.

Where do we get to see the events culminating in Armageddon’s Blade and the Sword of Frost clashing and destroying the world?

Enroth was getting blown up regardless of what happened. Greg Fulton said that they did it because the lore was getting too complicated to track across two different dev teams and they wanted a clean slate. It's the same reason the Might and Magic games mostly took place on different planets before HoMM became a smash hit.

Whether that was a good solution or not is... *debatable*, but that was the solution they came up with.

sames lmao, but we could get both

submission.title: Getting through Chronicles and

comments.body: As far as I’m concerned my adventures with Tarnum are the best ones I ever had in HOMM. Every one of them was worthy of multiple play throughs. lol

Chronicles and Heroes IV are easily the best-written games in the series. If you've never played IV, I'd recommend playing at least the Might campaign after you're done Chronicles, since it's a great epilogue to Tarnum's story.
As different as IV is, I enjoy playing to its differences from all the other games in the series.

conquest of the underground was the first campaign i played from start to finish. Apparently i didnt pick up wisdom or any schools of magic on tarnum, but then I picked up Cutbert and accidentally leveled him up properly. I was in awe watching him through chain lightnings and mass CC spells and I had NO IDEA why or how that was happening. 

Now lore wise, conquest of underground (to my knowledge) is the only piece of the lore where inferno is straight up portrayed as actual devils and demons instead of aliens. This inconsistency bothers me so much since i love that campaign, but if anyone can explain it away for me please go ahead
Cuthbert is my go-to ass kicker! Got very lucky in getting Destroy Undead in my first mages guild. Helped run through alot of the early game in alot of spots.

There's no real explanation; there are both actual demons and aliens that look like demons in the Might and Magic universe. For example, Heroes I and II both had a map object called a Daemon Cave where you encountered a non-Kreegan demon. It's just more pragmatic for the devs to reuse Inferno to portray the actual for-reals demons.

Because the serie is a continuity mess, there is nothing to explain. Another example is Crag Hack, it's a Dwarf barbarian from MM3, who crashed into Enroth, suddenly grow to human size, get in Celeste, but has nothing to do with Crag Hack, the human sized barbarian from Enroth.
Wait holy shit, Crag Hack was originally a dwarf? Is there anywhere I can find out about his appearance in MM3?
https://mightandmagic.fandom.com/wiki/Crag_Hack_(Terra) Note he is human sized (same as Resurrectra and Dark Shade, who are gnomes) in the intro of MM7.
I tried but Homm IV kept on crashing...
Wow, thanks. Crazy old games.
>**Gregory Fulton:** \[...\] I think it is safe to assume all demons in the **Jon Van Caneghem** era are Kreegans, until a different developer or another game effectively explains otherwise.

That's why when someone is saying that something is not "lore accurate" or "we have never seen X" or "Y is like that in the game Z", you should just laugh at him and tell him there is no coherence in any form in any of the NWC games. 


MM7 and HOMM 3 are supposed to be on the same continent, but the difference in lore and environment are so big it's straight up bullshit. There is zero continuity between NWC games.

Part of that issue is also the fact that when they were naming heroes in Homm 1 and Homm 2 they just grabbed names from earlier MM games, especially the Xeen one. Like Sandro was a lich in a dungeon, Crag Hack one of the pre-made party party members. 

On it's own no problem on having a few references among them, but the sheer number was a bit too much. And then when the story of MM7 was made they just decided to go with the premade names and didn't really bother hey what about Enroth Hack or Enroth Resurrecta.

Part of it and a major one of the mess of inconsistencies is the fact that it seems that the MM and HoMM teams barely interacted each other. At least this is one of the vibes I've gotten from few interviews. There seems to have been one person coordinating things, but otherwise there seems to have been no major, hey are we on the same page with it.
Let's go even crazier, Crag Hack is named after Jon Van Caneghems (the series creator) 1e AD&D character
Try with hd mod and gl wrapper. Fixes a lot of issues in the base game and make it playable on modern pc.

What’s the best way to play chronicles
Now? Isn’t there some mod that adds them into regular heroes 3 so you can use the Hd mod?
Currently I'm playing the GOG version. Haven't messed with any mods yet 🤔

My working assumption was both actual-demons-from-hell and space-aliens-called-Kreegans exist in the NWCverse, and that if this is confusing for us, just imagine what it's like for the people of Antagarich!

Even in the Inferno faction, I believe only a few of the creatures are actual Kreegans - Pit Lords and Devils, I think? But Imps, Hell Hounds, Gogs, etc., are something else, which would be consistent with the idea that there are 'real' demons separate from the Kreegans. This would also help explain why people immediately misidentified the Kreegans as demons when they came to Eeofol - they must have had some pre-existing knowledge of the concept.

Of course, OOC I suspect the answer is that *Heroes Chronicles*, like most of the NWC M&M games, was written as heroic fantasy pastiche first, integrated continuity second. Why are there demons in 'Conquest of the Underworld'? Because it would be cool to have a story where Tarnum literally invades Hell to rescue the soul of the man who defeated him, and that's the important thing!

submission.title: Olden Era - What do you look the most forward to?
submission.body:
With a brand new and promising Heroes game coming soon, what do you look forward to the most? Is there something particularly you’ve missed over the years?

Personally, I look forward to the unique factions the most, followed by the gameplay. I was really impressed with the factions in Heroes 5 and 6 and just hope they can still live up to those expectations.

That’s definitely not the case with HoMM2. Town Portal is very rare here, so you need more than one hero to be able to fight on several fronts in most maps. And in HoMM1, town portal doesn’t exist at all, only dimension door and town gate.

In HoMM3 you can get off easier using just one hero  in more cases, since town portal is so easy to learn, but whether you are playing for score, challenge or trying to finish a map quickly, using more than one fighting hero that levels up is a strong advantage over just one. HoMM3 incentivizes you to give the hardest hero battles to your best hero though, unless you are purposefully playing without using town portal and dimension door.

  
If you use the dimension door spell in HoMM2, I usually don’t, then you do have some of the same advantages as with town portal in HoMM3. But you still feel like you need other fighting heroes on the map, because using just one hero, is very suboptimal time wise, compared to using more.

This difference in how the games play, is one of several reasons why I think the gameplay ultimately is better in HoMM2 than in HoMM3.
Unfrozen also keeps saying "Early Access will not require UbiConnect to play" does that mean the official release will require UbiConnect? so I'm also looking forward to a good explanation.

Yes, this is a game that would be perfect for people that wishes to focus on one hero, although you can have many here too. But it is much more focused on exploration, RPG stuff, building up your heroes and battles, than the other games in the series. There is much less focus on what we think of as modern strategy game mechanics here to worry about, as the AI is so dumb and passive.

King’s Bounty from 1990 is also a game I can highly recommend. For the most part it isn’t as detailed in its mechanics as HoMM4, especially not with hero development. But it is a very unique and very fun game, where you use just one hero on four interlinked maps, and there are no other heroes moving around, only static villains that you have to locate, and “wandering monsters“ that you must fight or flee from. The mechanics are quite similar to HOMM mechanics in many ways, but also very different, so playing it felt like rediscovering HoMM all over again, when I did so a few years ago.

Unlike the HoMM series, the graphics aren’t anything special, and you don’t want to listen to the PC speaker sound or music in the game, so you turn that off, and instead play a fitting playlist. For me a mix of seventies hard rock and 80s metal albums with fantasy lyrics did the trick the first times I played the game, but using a randomized playlist of all the HoMM soundtracks also does the trick.

But the gameplay is pure joy, at least for me, so I replayed it two times right after I finished it for the first time, and have went back to it later as well. It is just such a quick, replayable and open ended game. Very much in the Jon Van Caneghem game design philosophy, like the old HoMM and Might and Magic games.

In addition there’s also the “new” King’s Bounty from the early 2000s, and the sequel which is called  Armored Princess or something like that. I’ve never played these games, but they seem to be quite popular. Like HoMM5 is to the old HoMM games, these were made by a Russian or Slavic team that were fans of the original King’s Bounty. They seem like fun games, but I will say that they also seem to be much slower, more repetitive and less open ended than the original King’s Bounty. So in many ways completely different games, although they share probably a lot of the same basic design.

You should give 2 or 1 a try as well. They have many similarities, but they play very differently to HoMM3. And the faction rooster is very different also.

HoMM1 lacks a couple of niceties you find in later games, of which the lack of visibility of how far your units can move in the battlefield is the most egregious one. But it has some of the most charming visuals in the series, and a unique take on the spell system, that pushes you to rely less on the same spells over and over.

I mostly agree about what you say about the visuals, though something actually looking inventive and original would have been very cool as well.

I feel like the visual design in strategy games have regressed a lot in the last decades compared to in the 90s. While you see a lot of cool visual design in other genres, especially in some indie games, there is a major problem with the game industry that is especially visible in strategy games.

Before, it used to be that the people who did the visual design for computer games where mostly inspired by other media outside of games, because the medium was so fresh, and also under rapid development. These days, the most important inspiration for visual artists seem to be other games, which gives the visual design in many games a very regurgitated feeling. This is especially apparent in computer strategy games, where the Warcraft style has been completely dominant for two decades now, and you hardly see a strategy game that isn’t influenced by it.

I personally dislike this style a lot, with all the deformed bodies, bicepses larger than heads, unpractical, overembelished armor and weapons, and fluorescent lights everywhere. Especially on undead creatures. The visual design of HoMM 5-7 is especially burdened by this, but it is also highly visible in for example the latest Age of Wonders game, or even this new HoMM game, which tries to be original, but also has a lot of Warcraft-elements to it.

I wouldn’t say HoMM3 didn’t have faction identity at all, but I think I get what you are saying. You want more of a faction identity centered on different races and their allies. Age of Wonders 1 and Age of Wonders: Shadow Magic is very strong on this, with a really wide variety of units and abilities, but also a faction identity that makes a lot of sense.

Especially in Age of Wonders 1, where you really identified with the race you played as, when you played them. It is a bit different in Shadow Magic, since that game feels more like a struggle between godlike wizards manipulating the races under their control, than a struggle of different civilizations/races themselves. So in that case more like Master of Magic or even Heroes of Might and Magic.

Age of Wonders 3 has some of this as well, but in that game your unit, abilities and identity is more of a fusion between the race and the hero type you pick. It has more modern, but in my opinion not as good looking visuals as the older games, if you care about that.

submission.title: What is the best way to play HoMM 3?
submission.body:
Played the game for a little bit as a young kid but 23 years later I'd like to play it properly. 

What's the best way to go about it and get the best experience possible? With or with expansion.  Just got a new Pc, haven't played anything on pc for years and always been a console player.

comments.body:
First off, you need the GOG version. It's the game with its expansions and the only way you have to get everything AFAIK.

The game runs just fine as-is, but VCMI have a lot of improvements on it, the most interesting being a better high resolution support, however it also changes stuff like AI if I'm not mistaken, so it's really.up to you to decide.

Lastly a lot of people swear by the unofficial expansion Horn of the abyss, I haven't tried it personally so I can't vow for it but it seems to be considered a must have nowadays

The [GOG.com](http://GOG.com) version is by far the best. It has both expansions, and a robust modding community. I'd also recommend installing the HD mod to go with it, since it comes with major quality of life improvements.

Whatever you do, avoid the Steam release. It's a cash grab that's missing half the original features and barely supports any QoL mods.
This is the way, Gog and hd+
Honestly, the GOG version with the expansions is the best. The Horn of the Abyss mod is not a must, but it’s pretty damn good and does give some nice gameplay improvements.
Thanks for the responses! I appreciate it. I will go ahead with the GOG version.
and also kills several exploits
Sure, but for someone who is coming back to the game after 23 years, exploits might not be their biggest issue😄
Happy gaming!
Enjoy! And whenever you want some additional content, check the Horn of the Abyss unofficial expansion. It's fantastic and very well balanced into the game.
Complete edition on Gog, then I recommend modding in the Hota expansion
The Steam release is also missing the Armageddon’s Blade & Shadow of Death expansions. So avoid buying it
Here to second the GOG version. They also have the Chronicles chapters which I'm currently playing and they're great!

I'd recommend getting Might and Magic 8. There's a mod for 8 that combines 6, 7 and 8 into a single game, and those three (the "Enroth trilogy") are the New World Computing Might and Magic games with the strongest connection to Olden Era and New World Computing's Heroes of Might and Magic, and also arguably the Might and Magic games that've aged the best.

Because Ygdrasa (mother of the Arslegard gods) and the Wyrdes, who were supposed to be the Guardians of Axeoth, got corrupted, forgot their purpose and history as agents of the Ancients and began just tinkering with Axeoth's culture (per Tim Lang, the developer).
Plus the humans on VARN-4, Enroth and all the other Ancient seedings come from the same ancestral stock presumably, so "alien" only applies in the sense of "foreigner" here.

It's mostly in the backstory. The series takes place on planets and artificial environments that were seeded by a spacefaring race called the Ancients and then lost touch with them and their technology because the gate network was attacked by the Kreegans (the Inferno town demons). Not unlike the *Warcraft* universe in several respects, where you have the Titans seeding many worlds and being fought by the demons of the Burning Legion.

In the RPG *Might and Magic* games, you usually end up doing something related to leftover Ancient tech by the end of the game. The articles about the series and individual games on Wikipedia and TV Tropes Wiki are pretty informative if you want to check them out.

submission.title: Ranking factions based on how likely they are to appear in Olden Era DLC
submission.body:
**DEFINETELY NOT**

**Empire:** Literally noone remembers it exists, it appeared only once in a spinoff. Why the hell would they bring it back????

**Conflux:** We already know Conflux will be partially in Sylvan town. Even one of their campaign heroes will be a fire elemental. Conflux also was very unfinished at release so it gained pretty negative reputation, that they would not want in the Olden Era.

**Sanctuary:** It's the old universe. Nagas are not a separate faction.

**PROBABLY NOT**

**Fortress (Dwarven):** Despite first appearing in Ashan, I still think it has better chances to appear in Olden Era than Sanctuary. Dwarfs are actually a separate faction in the Old Universe, so I can see them appearing. I still think they won't because they are not something we have seen in the Old Universe.

**Academy/Tower:** The secret 6th faction already seems to be largely inspired by it, even if they are not the same thing. Like, even their hero looks like a generic mage that would fit in the Tower faction.

**MAYBE**

**Fortress (Swamp):** It feels like this is a town that people miss the most. It would just make perfect sense to bring it back... but there is one problem. Inferno already has similiar insect themes. I don't think it's that big of a deal, but I can see it as a reason for it to not come back.

**Cove:** It appeared few times in spinoffs, but I feel like it has a chance to come back. People love Cove, it would make sense to make it appear in a semi-official main game.

**HIGH CHANCES TO APPEAR**

**Stronghold:** It appeared in every game so far, why would it loose the streak now? I'm not a huge fan of the faction, but it's so beloved that it would be a shame if it didn't appear.

comments.body:
Fortress represented the nation of tatalia, which was in erathia, the game is set in another continent, so no.

Conflux does make sense in the lore, it was just disliked due to poor balance, not because of the concept.

Tower may appear, as wizards are a staple, but itll not be bracada.
While Fortress represented the nation of Tatalia, there is a lizardfolk population native to Jadame with a similar style of architecture to the Tatalians.

If they do a Dwarven faction, them being Dark Dwarves and strongly tied to the Earth Elementals would give it a bit of a different spin, so there is that argument in favour (of course, conversely what they did to the Dark Elves is an indication Ashan-style trumps Jadame-veracity).  
The other Fortress, Cove and Stronghold have the advantage of also having canonical Jadamean groups that do or could associate with the towns in areas on that map they posted - lizardfolk on Dagger Wound, trolls in Ironsand, the piratical Empire of the Endless Ocean on Regna (another element of the map indicates Olden Era takes place after the pirates displaced the trolls).

The very first area in M&M 8, where this continent Jadame first appeared, literally started you off in a lizardman village. They are literally your first look at Jadame besides the games intro cutscene.
I stand corrected, i admit my knowledge of lore is mostly from heroes 3.

Assuming the game is successful enough for DLC, I would put Stronghold at a 100% chance. Like you said, it's been in every game. I would be absolutely gobsmacked if it's not planned as the first town to be added.

I would actually put some combination of Cove/HIII Fortress as the #2 likeliest inclusion, specifically due to the lore of where OE takes place. Apparently the Regnan pirates and Lizardfolk are both strongly present in the M&M game that takes place on Jadame, so doing some kind of swamp town which mashes up Cove and Fortress feels like a very natural inclusion.

Similar insect theme? Fortress has one insect creature which also has zero heroes of it.

Between the lizardmen, the basilisks, wyverns, hydra and even the gorgons having quite reptilian looking scales. Surely the theme is much more with lizards than insects?

Stronghold is extremely likely, so likely I bet my right arm that they're going to add it in early access like they've hinted at a few times. Stronghold is the only non-faction that had a city highlighted on the map of Jadame they showed off (that coliseum to the northeast is Zog's Fortress, an ogre dungeon in MM8).

Cove is also quite high on the list, since its homeland, Regna, is that crescent island above the mainland of Jadame.
